title: Effect
description: An Effect describes what changes when an operation successfully executes — state transitions, derived updates, and side effects like notifications or downstream operations.

operation.description: The operation this effect applies to, expressed as Resource.Action.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this effect does.
examples: Marks the loan as active and records the approval timestamp.
Cancels the order and initiates a payment refund.

changes.description: Attribute changes applied to the resource after the operation executes.

set.description: The value to assign to the attribute. Use 'now' for the current timestamp.

triggers.description: Downstream operations to initiate after this operation completes.

examples.description: Marks the loan as active and records the approval timestamp.
Marks the order as cancelled and triggers a payment refund.
Marks the employee as inactive and records their end date.
